User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Dermaflow AI application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

creationDate.description: The timestamp for when the user created the account.

lastLogin.description: The timestamp for when the user last logged in

languagePreference.description: The user's preferred language for the app interface and voice readouts.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership for simplified security rules. Includes id, email, creationDate, lastLogin, and languagePreference.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. User data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring path-based ownership, which simplifies security rules. The application will store user data in a dedicated collection.  There are no collaborative data models that require membership maps. This design supports secure `list` operations by ensuring all documents within a collection share the same security requirements. There are no global roles. Authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid`. Timestamps will be used for creation and modification times. This enforces the invariants of ownership and timestamps.
